iifym.name: IIFYM (If It Fits Your Macros)
iifym.description: Flexible dieting focusing on hitting daily macronutrient targets (Protein, Carbs, Fat) rather than restricting specific foods.
iifym.best_for: People wanting food freedom, sustainable long-term dieting.
iifym.how_it_works: Calculate TDEE, set a calorie goal, assign protein (e.g. 2g/kg), fat (e.g. 0.8g/kg), and fill the rest with carbs. Eat whatever you want as long as you hit those numbers.
iifym.sample_schedule: No fixed schedule. Eat when convenient.
iifym.pros: Highly sustainable, prevents binge eating, allows social eating.
iifym.cons: Can lead to micronutrient deficiencies if abused with junk food. Requires meticulous tracking.

clean eating.name: Clean Eating
clean eating.description: Focuses on consuming whole, unprocessed, nutrient-dense foods. Avoids refined sugars and artificial additives.
clean eating.best_for: Overall health, improving digestion, micronutrient density.
clean eating.how_it_works: Base diet around vegetables, lean meats, whole grains, fruits, nuts, and seeds.
clean eating.cons: Can be restrictive, difficult for social events, 'clean' foods still have calories causing unexpected weight gain if portions aren't managed.

intermittent fasting.name: Intermittent Fasting (16:8)
intermittent fasting.description: Eating pattern cycling between periods of fasting and eating.
intermittent fasting.best_for: People who prefer larger meals, controlling late-night snacking.
intermittent fasting.how_it_works: Fast for 16 hours, eat all calories within an 8-hour window.
intermittent fasting.sample_schedule: Fast from 8 PM to 12 PM next day. Eat lunch at 12 PM, snack at 4 PM, dinner at 7:30 PM.
intermittent fasting.pros: Simplifies eating, can improve insulin sensitivity, helps maintain calorie deficit easily.
intermittent fasting.cons: Can cause low energy in mornings, difficult to hit high protein goals for muscle gain.

carb cycling.name: Carb Cycling
carb cycling.description: Alternating high carb and low carb days, usually aligned with training intensity.
carb cycling.best_for: Advanced athletes, breaking fat loss plateaus while maintaining performance.
carb cycling.how_it_works: High carb days on heavy training days (legs/back) to fuel performance. Low carb days on rest or light cardio days to enhance fat burning.
carb cycling.sample_schedule: Mon (Legs): High Carb, Tue (Upper): Moderate Carb, Wed (Rest): Low Carb.
carb cycling.pros: Maximizes gym performance while losing fat, improves insulin sensitivity.
carb cycling.cons: Complex to plan and track, requires strict adherence.

ketogenic.name: Ketogenic Diet
ketogenic.description: Very low carb, high fat diet designed to put the body into a state of ketosis.
ketogenic.best_for: People with insulin resistance, sedentary individuals, those who prefer high-fat foods.
ketogenic.how_it_works: Restrict carbs to <30g per day. Body switches to burning ketones (fat) for fuel instead of glucose.
ketogenic.sample_schedule: Eggs/Bacon for breakfast, Salad with olive oil/chicken for lunch, Salmon with buttery asparagus for dinner.
ketogenic.pros: Strong appetite suppression, rapid initial water weight loss.
ketogenic.cons: Poor for high-intensity anaerobic performance (lifting), 'keto flu' during adaptation, highly restrictive.

high protein low carb.name: High Protein Low Carb
high protein low carb.description: Similar to keto but with more protein and slightly more carbs, avoiding full ketosis.
high protein low carb.best_for: Fat loss while preserving muscle, managing hunger.
high protein low carb.how_it_works: Protein is kept very high (>2.2g/kg). Carbs kept low (50-100g) mainly from vegetables.
high protein low carb.sample_schedule: Omelette for breakfast, Chicken salad for lunch, Paneer tikka and veggies for dinner.
high protein low carb.pros: Great for muscle retention during cuts, highly satiating.
high protein low carb.cons: Can leave you feeling flat in the gym due to low glycogen.

calorie cycling.name: Calorie Cycling
calorie cycling.description: Fluctuating daily calorie intake while maintaining a weekly average target.
calorie cycling.best_for: Social flexibility, enjoying weekends without ruining diet.
calorie cycling.how_it_works: Eat lower calories Mon-Thu, eat higher calories Fri-Sun, but weekly average remains in a deficit.
calorie cycling.cons: Easy to overeat on high days and ruin the weekly deficit.

reverse dieting.name: Reverse Dieting
reverse dieting.description: Slowly increasing calories after a prolonged diet to restore metabolism while minimizing fat gain.
reverse dieting.best_for: Post-diet transition to maintenance, recovering from metabolic adaptation.
reverse dieting.how_it_works: Add 50-100 calories (mostly carbs/fat) each week until maintenance is reached.
reverse dieting.pros: Restores hormonal balance and metabolic rate without rebound weight gain.
reverse dieting.cons: Requires immense patience and strict tracking.

maintenance.name: Maintenance Phase
maintenance.description: Eating at TDEE to maintain current body weight.
maintenance.best_for: Consolidating gains or fat loss, taking a break from dieting, performance phases.
maintenance.how_it_works: Eat exactly what you burn. Focus shifts to performance and recovery.
maintenance.pros: Sustainable, good energy levels, mental break.
maintenance.cons: No rapid changes in body weight.

lean bulk.name: Lean Bulk
lean bulk.description: A slight caloric surplus aimed at maximizing muscle gain while minimizing fat accumulation.
lean bulk.how_it_works: Eat 200-300 calories above maintenance. Ensure adequate protein.
lean bulk.sample_schedule: 3 solid meals + 1 pre-workout snack + 1 protein shake.
lean bulk.pros: Builds muscle with minimal fat gain, easy to transition out of.
lean bulk.cons: Muscle gain is slower than a dirty bulk.

dirty bulk.name: Dirty Bulk
dirty bulk.description: A large caloric surplus with no food restrictions to maximize weight gain.
dirty bulk.best_for: Hardgainers, powerlifters not in a weight class, absolute mass building.
dirty bulk.how_it_works: Eat 500-1000+ calories above maintenance. Eat whatever is necessary to hit calorie goals.
dirty bulk.sample_schedule: Large meals, liquid calories, high-calorie snacks.
dirty bulk.cons: Significant fat gain, potential negative impacts on health markers (lipids, insulin).

mini cut.name: Mini Cut
mini cut.description: A short, aggressive fat loss phase used during a bulking cycle.
mini cut.best_for: Stripping off fat mid-bulk to extend the bulking runway.
mini cut.how_it_works: Aggressive deficit (500-800 cal) for 2-6 weeks to drop fat fast without losing muscle.
mini cut.sample_schedule: High protein, very low carb/fat for 4 weeks.
mini cut.pros: Quick results, clears up insulin sensitivity, lets you get back to bulking sooner.

muscle gain 2500 veg.description: Vegetarian muscle building plan prioritizing paneer, whey, and lentils for protein.
muscle gain 2500 veg.notes: Drink 3-4 liters of water. Adjust rice/roti quantities to hit exact calories.

muscle gain 2800 non-veg.description: High protein non-veg plan utilizing eggs, chicken breast, and whey.
muscle gain 2800 non-veg.notes: Cook chicken with minimal oil. Use spices freely for taste.

fat loss 1600 veg.description: Aggressive fat loss for vegetarians, high volume foods to maintain satiety.

meals.name: Dinner
foods: 150g Tofu Stir Fry with Broccoli and Bell Peppers

fat loss 1600 veg.notes: Drink black coffee or green tea to manage hunger.

fat loss 1800 non-veg.description: Sustainable fat loss plan utilizing lean meats for high satiety.
fat loss 1800 non-veg.notes: Protein is kept very high to prevent muscle loss during the calorie deficit.

maintenance 2000.notes: A good baseline to adjust upwards for bulking or downwards for cutting.

athletic performance 3000.description: High carb diet to fuel intense training sessions.
athletic performance 3000.notes: Carbs are placed strategically around the workout for maximum energy and recovery.

budget muscle gain.description: Cheap, high-calorie, adequate protein plan relying on eggs, dal, and peanuts.
budget muscle gain.notes: Extremely cost-effective. Peanuts provide dense calories and healthy fats.

creatine.name: Creatine Monohydrate
creatine.benefit: Increases strength, power output, and muscle volume by replenishing ATP stores.
creatine.caution: Drink adequate water to prevent cramping.

whey protein.name: Whey Protein
whey protein.benefit: Convenient, high-quality, fast-absorbing protein source to hit daily macro goals.
whey protein.timing: Post-workout or anytime you need protein.
whey protein.caution: May cause bloating in lactose intolerant individuals (use Isolate instead).

caffeine.name: Caffeine
caffeine.benefit: Central nervous system stimulant, decreases perceived exertion, increases focus.
caffeine.caution: Avoid within 6-8 hours of sleep. Can cause jitters.

beta-alanine.name: Beta-Alanine
beta-alanine.benefit: Buffers lactic acid, improving endurance in the 1-4 minute rep range (e.g., high rep sets).

omega-3.name: Omega-3 Fish Oil
omega-3.benefit: Reduces inflammation, supports cardiovascular and joint health.

vitamin d.name: Vitamin D3
vitamin d.benefit: Supports bone health, immune function, and optimal testosterone levels.
vitamin d.caution: Toxicity is rare but possible at massive doses.

magnesium.name: Magnesium
magnesium.benefit: Supports nerve function, muscle relaxation, and sleep quality.
magnesium.caution: Avoid Magnesium Oxide (poor absorption), use Citrate, Glycinate, or Threonate.

zinc.name: Zinc
zinc.benefit: Supports immune system and testosterone production (if deficient).
zinc.caution: Taking on an empty stomach causes severe nausea.

bcaas.name: BCAAs (Branched-Chain Amino Acids)
bcaas.benefit: May prevent muscle breakdown during fasted training. Largely redundant if eating enough total protein.
bcaas.caution: Often a waste of money if eating adequate whey/meat.

pre-workout.name: Pre-Workout Blends
pre-workout.benefit: Combines caffeine, pump ingredients (citrulline), and focus agents.
pre-workout.caution: Watch total caffeine intake. Do not dry scoop.

casein.name: Casein Protein
casein.benefit: Slow-digesting protein providing a steady stream of amino acids.

collagen.name: Collagen Peptides
collagen.benefit: May support joint, tendon, skin, and hair health.
collagen.timing: Daily, preferably with Vitamin C 60 mins before exercise.
collagen.caution: Not a complete protein, doesn't count towards muscle building protein.

ashwagandha.name: Ashwagandha
ashwagandha.benefit: Adaptogen that reduces cortisol (stress hormone) and may modestly boost testosterone.
ashwagandha.caution: May cause lethargy in some people. Cycle it (e.g., 2 months on, 1 month off).

melatonin.name: Melatonin
melatonin.best_for: Shift workers, jet lag, occasional insomnia.
melatonin.caution: High doses (>5mg) can cause grogginess the next day.

multivitamin.name: Multivitamin
multivitamin.benefit: Fills potential micronutrient gaps in the diet.
multivitamin.caution: Does not replace a diet rich in fruits and vegetables.

calorie deficit.name: Calorie Deficit
calorie deficit.description: Consuming fewer calories than you burn. The absolute requirement for fat loss.
common_mistakes: Cutting calories too drastically, leading to muscle loss.

protein sparing.name: Protein Sparing
protein sparing.description: Keeping protein exceptionally high during a cut to preserve lean mass.
protein sparing.how_to: Eat 2.2-2.7g of protein per kg of bodyweight.
protein sparing.best_for: Aggressive cuts, lean individuals trying to get shredded.

diet breaks.name: Diet Breaks
diet breaks.description: Taking 1-2 weeks at maintenance calories during a long cut.
diet breaks.how_to: After 6-8 weeks of cutting, eat at TDEE for 1 week.
common_mistakes: Turning a diet break into a binge week.

refeed days.name: Refeed Days
refeed days.description: A single day of eating at maintenance, entirely via increased carbohydrates.
refeed days.how_to: Keep fats low, protein moderate, and increase carbs heavily for one day.
refeed days.best_for: Replenishing muscle glycogen, boosting leptin levels.
common_mistakes: Eating junk food (high fat/sugar) instead of complex carbs.

water cutting.name: Water Cutting
water cutting.description: Temporarily manipulating water and sodium to drop scale weight.
water cutting.how_to: Water load for 3 days, then cut water 12 hours before weigh-in.
common_mistakes: Doing this for aesthetic reasons—it's dangerous and temporary.

sodium manipulation.name: Sodium Manipulation
sodium manipulation.description: Keeping sodium constant to prevent water weight fluctuations.
sodium manipulation.how_to: Eat roughly the same amount of salt daily.
common_mistakes: Cutting sodium entirely, leading to flat muscles and cramps.

cardio stacking.name: Cardio Stacking
cardio stacking.description: Gradually adding cardio as weight loss stalls, rather than cutting more food.
cardio stacking.how_to: Start with 0 cardio. When weight stalls, add 2x20 mins. Stall again, add 3x20 mins.
common_mistakes: Doing 1 hour of cardio daily from Day 1.

lean bulk.name: Lean Bulk
lean bulk.best_for: Minimizing fat gain while building muscle.

dirty bulk.name: Dirty Bulk
dirty bulk.best_for: Underweight beginners who struggle to eat.
common_mistakes: Gaining too much fat, making the inevitable cut miserable.

calorie surplus sizing.name: Calorie Surplus Sizing
calorie surplus sizing.description: Adjusting surplus size based on experience level.
calorie surplus sizing.best_for: Aligning food intake with actual muscle growth potential.
common_mistakes: Advanced lifters trying to bulk like beginners.

progressive overload nutrition.name: Progressive Overload Nutrition
progressive overload nutrition.description: Increasing food intake as body weight goes up.
progressive overload nutrition.how_to: When scale stalls for 2 weeks on a bulk, add 150 calories.
common_mistakes: Eating the same calories even after gaining 5kg.

peri-workout nutrition.name: Peri-Workout Nutrition
peri-workout nutrition.description: Concentrating carbs around the training window.
peri-workout nutrition.how_to: Eat 40% of daily carbs pre-workout and 40% post-workout.
common_mistakes: Over-complicating it—total daily intake matters most.

muscle memory.name: Muscle Memory
muscle memory.description: Regaining lost muscle is much faster than building new muscle.
muscle memory.how_to: Eat at a slight surplus and train hard; muscle returns rapidly.
muscle memory.best_for: People returning to the gym after a long break.
common_mistakes: Dirty bulking thinking the rapid weight gain is entirely new muscle.

pre-workout meal.name: Pre-Workout Meal
pre-workout meal.description: Fueling the body for an intense session.
pre-workout meal.what_to_eat: Complex carbs + moderate protein + low fat.
pre-workout meal.why: To top off glycogen stores without causing gastrointestinal distress.

post-workout meal.name: Post-Workout Meal
post-workout meal.description: Kickstarting the recovery and muscle protein synthesis process.
post-workout meal.why: To replenish glycogen and provide amino acids for repair.

pre-sleep nutrition.name: Pre-Sleep Nutrition
pre-sleep nutrition.why: To provide a trickle of amino acids while fasting overnight.

intra-workout.name: Intra-Workout
intra-workout.when: During workouts lasting longer than 90 minutes.
intra-workout.why: To prevent glycogen depletion in marathon sessions.

carb backloading.name: Carb Backloading
carb backloading.description: Eating majority of daily carbs in the evening.
carb backloading.why: Based on the theory that insulin sensitivity is higher post-workout, avoiding fat storage.
